blocks:
Sūrat Qāf

Makkan, and it is 45 verses.

Sūrat Qāf

Makkan, and it is forty-five verses by consensus. بِسْمِ اللهِ الرَّحْمنِ الرَّحِيمِ

(In the Name of Allah, the All-beneficent, the All-merciful)

(١) ق وَالْقُرْآنِ الْمَجِيدِ. (1) Qāf. By the glorious Quran! In al-Ma'ani, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): As for ** ق , it is the mountain (الجبل) surrounding the earth, and the greenness of the sky is from it. And by it, Allah holds the earth from shaking with its inhabitants. And al-Qummi said: ** ق ** is a mountain (جبل) surrounding the world from beyond Gog and Magog (يأجوج ومأجوج), and it is an oath (قسم).

(٢) بَلْ عَجِبُوا ** (2) Rather they are astonished Al-Qummi said: It means Quraysh. أَنْ جاءَهُمْ مُنْذِرٌ مِنْهُمْ ** (that a warner has come to them from among themselves) He said: It means the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله. Then the disbelievers said: "This is a wondrous thing (شَيْءٌ عَجِيبٌ)."

(٣) أَإِذا مِتْنا وَكُنَّا تُراباً ** (3) 'What, when we are dead and become dust [shall we be resurrected]? That is a far-fetched return!' Meaning: Shall we return when we have died and become dust? ذلِكَ رَجْعٌ بَعِيدٌ ** (That is a far-fetched return!) Al-Qummi said: It was revealed concerning Ubayy ibn Khalaf. He said to Abu Jahl: "Come to me so that I may astonish you concerning Muhammad صلى الله عليه وآله." Then he took a bone and crushed it, and then said: "O Muhammad, you claim that this will be brought back to life (يَحْيَى)."

(٤) قَدْ عَلِمْنا ما تَنْقُصُ الْأَرْضُ مِنْهُمْ ** (We certainly know what the earth diminishes of them) what it consumes of the bodies of their dead ** وَعِنْدَنا كِتابٌ حَفِيظٌ ** (and with Us is a preserving Book) preserving the details of all things, or preserved from change. (٥) بَلْ كَذَّبُوا بِالْحَقِّ لَمَّا جاءَهُمْ فَهُمْ فِي أَمْرٍ مَرِيجٍ ** (Rather, they denied the truth when it came to them, so they are in a state of confusion) confused (مضطرب); sometimes they say that he is a poet, sometimes that he is a sorcerer, and sometimes that he is a soothsayer, and so on. (٦) أَفَلَمْ يَنْظُرُوا ** (Have they not regarded) when they disbelieved in the Resurrection ** إِلَى السَّماءِ فَوْقَهُمْ ** (the sky above them) the signs of Allah's power in the creation of the universe ** كَيْفَ بَنَيْناها ** (how We built it) We raised it without pillars ** وَزَيَّنَّاها ** (and adorned it) with the stars ** وَما لَها مِنْ فُرُوجٍ ** (and it has no fissures) cracks (فُتوق), by creating it smooth and with layers adhering closely together.

(٧) وَالْأَرْضَ مَدَدْناها We spread it out (بَسَطْنَاهَا). وَأَلْقَيْنا فِيها رَواسِيَ Firm, stable mountains (جِبَالًا ثَوَابِتَ). وَأَنْبَتْنا فِيها مِنْ كُلِّ زَوْجٍ بَهِيجٍ From every beautiful kind (مِنْ كُلِّ صِنْفٍ حَسَنٍ). (7) And the earth—We have spread it out, and cast in it firm mountains, and We have grown in it every delightful kind [of vegetation]. (٨) تَبْصِرَةً وَذِكْرى لِكُلِّ عَبْدٍ مُنِيبٍ Returning to his Lord (رَاجِعٌ إِلَى رَبِّهِ), reflecting upon the wonders of His creation (مُتَفَكِّرٌ فِي بَدَائِعِ صُنْعِهِ). (8) [This is] for an insight and a reminder for every penitent servant. (٩) وَنَزَّلْنا مِنَ السَّماءِ ماءً مُبارَكاً Abundant in benefits (كَثِيرُ الْمَنَافِعِ). (9) And We sent down from the sky blessed water, In al-Kafi, narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him), he said: The Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله said regarding this Ayah: "There is no water on the earth except that the water of the sky has mixed with it." ** فَأَنْبَتْنا بِهِ جَنَّاتٍ Trees or fruits (أَشْجَارٌ أَوْ ثِمَارٌ). وَحَبَّ الْحَصِيدِ And the grain of the crops (وَحَبُّ الزَّرْعِ) which is meant to be harvested, such as wheat (الْبُرّ) and barley (الشَّعِير). (9) ... and grew thereby gardens and the grain to be harvested,

(١٠) وَالنَّخْلَ باسِقاتٍ ** (10) and tall date palms, Tall (طوالاً) or bearing fruit (حوامل). And singling them out for mention is due to their extreme height and the abundance of their benefits. لَها طَلْعٌ نَضِيدٌ ** having clustered spathes, Arranged (منضود), some of it above others. (١١) رِزْقاً لِلْعِبادِ وَأَحْيَيْنا بِهِ ** (11) as a provision for the servants, and We revived thereby By that water. بَلْدَةً مَيْتاً ** a dead land. A barren land (أرضاً جدبة) in which there is no growth (لا نماء فيها). كَذلِكَ الْخُرُوجُ ** So will be the emergence. Just as We sent down water from the sky, and brought forth by it vegetation from the earth, and revived by it the dead land, so will be your emergence, alive after your death. And this is an answer to their saying: أَإِذا مِتْنا وَكُنَّا تُراباً ذلِكَ رَجْعٌ بَعِيدٌ.** (Is it) when we are dead and have become dust, that is a far-fetched return?

(١٢) كَذَّبَتْ قَبْلَهُمْ قَوْمُ نُوحٍ وَأَصْحابُ الرَّسِ ** (12) Before them the people of Noah, and the People of the Well, denied [the truth], Those who buried (رسّوا) their prophet in the earth, meaning, they buried (رسّوه) him, as their story previously appeared in Surat al-Furqan. (١٣) وثمود وَعادٌ وَفِرْعَوْنُ ** (13) and Thamud, and ‘Ad, and Pharaoh, He intended him (Pharaoh) and his people so that it conforms to what precedes it and what follows it: وَإِخْوانُ لُوطٍ. (and the brethren of Lot.) (١٤) وَأَصْحابُ الْأَيْكَةِ ** (14) and the People of the Thicket, The thicket (الغيضة), and they are the people of Shu'ayb, as previously mentioned in Surat al-Hijr, وَقَوْمُ تُبَّعٍ (and the people of Tubba‘), as His mention previously appeared in Surat ad-Dukhan. كُلٌّ كَذَّبَ الرُّسُلَ فَحَقَّ وَعِيدِ (Every one denied the apostles, so My threat was realized.) So My threat (وعيدي) became necessary and descended upon them. And in this is a consolation for the Messenger (صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم) and a threat to them.

(١٥) أَفَعَيِينا بِالْخَلْقِ الْأَوَّلِ ** (Did We then fail in the first creation?) Did We fail in the initiation [of creation] so that We should fail in the repetition [of creation]? ** بَلْ هُمْ فِي لَبْسٍ مِنْ خَلْقٍ جَدِيدٍ ** (Rather, they are in confusion about a new creation.) Meaning: They do not deny Our power over the first creation, but rather they are in confusion (خَلْط) and doubt (شُبْهَة) concerning a renewed creation (خَلْقٍ مُسْتَأْنَف) because it involves contravening custom (مُخَالَفَة الْعَادَة). And the indefiniteness [of the word خَلْقٍ] is for magnification (لِلتَّعْظِيم) and to indicate that it is in a manner that is neither customary (مُتَعَارَف) nor habitual (مُعْتَاد).

In al-Tawhid, narrated from al-Baqir عليه السلام: He was asked about this Ayah, and He said: The interpretation (تَأْوِيل) of that is that when Allah Almighty annihilates this creation and this world, and the people of Paradise dwell in Paradise and the people of the Fire dwell in the Fire, Allah will renew a world other than this world, and He will renew a creation without males (فُحُولَة) or females (إِنَاث) who will worship Him and affirm His Oneness (يُوَحِّدُونَهُ). And He will create for them an earth other than this earth to carry them, and a heaven other than this heaven to shade them. Perhaps you think that Allah only created this one world, and you think that Allah did not create humans other than you. Nay, by Allah! He has created a thousand thousand worlds and a thousand thousand Adams. You are in the last of those worlds and those Adams.

In al-Khisal and al-Ayyashi, narrated from Him عليه السلام: [There is] that which is close to it, and it has already passed in Surat Ibrahim عليه السلام.

(١٦) وَلَقَدْ خَلَقْنَا الْإِنْسانَ وَنَعْلَمُ ما تُوَسْوِسُ بِهِ نَفْسُهُ ** (16) Certainly We created man, and We know what his soul whispers to him, What his soul speaks to him, and it is what crosses the mind. And al-waswasah (الوسوسة) is the hidden sound. وَنَحْنُ أَقْرَبُ إِلَيْهِ مِنْ حَبْلِ الْوَرِيدِ ** (and We are nearer to him than the jugular vein.) Al-Habl (الحبل) is the vein (العرق), and its attribution (إضافة) is for clarification. And the two warīd (الوريدان) are two veins surrounding the two sides of the neck in its front part, connected to the watīn (الوتين), returning to it from the head. And ḥabl al-warīd (حبل الوريد) is a metaphor for closeness.

(١٧) إِذْ يَتَلَقَّى الْمُتَلَقِّيانِ ** (17) When the two receivers receive [his deeds], When the two guardians (الحفيظان) receive what he utters. And in this is an indication that He is independent of the preservation (استحفاظ) by the two angels, for He is more knowing than them and aware of what is hidden from them, because He is nearer to him than them. But He did this for a wisdom that necessitated it, such as emphasizing the restraint of the servant from disobedience, and confirming the consideration of deeds and their recording for the recompense, and establishing the proof (الحجّة) on the Day the witnesses stand. عَنِ الْيَمِينِ وَعَنِ الشِّمالِ قَعِيدٌ.** (seated on the right and on the left.)

(١٨) ما يَلْفِظُ مِنْ قَوْلٍ إِلَّا لَدَيْهِ رَقِيبٌ ** (He does not utter any statement but there is with him an observer) an angel who observes his deed عَتِيدٌ ** (ready.) prepared, present. In al-Kafi, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): He said: There is no heart except that it has two ears. On one of them is a guiding angel (ملك مرشد), and on the other is a tempting devil (شيطان مفتن). This one commands him, and that one restrains him. The devil commands him with acts of disobedience (بالمعاصي), and the angel restrains him from them. And the Almighty's saying: عَنِ الْيَمِينِ وَعَنِ الشِّمالِ قَعِيدٌ ما يَلْفِظُ مِنْ قَوْلٍ إِلَّا لَدَيْهِ رَقِيبٌ عَتِيدٌ. (seated on the right and on the left. He does not utter any statement but there is with him an observer, ready.) In al-Jawami', narrated from the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله: He said: The writer of good deeds (كاتب الحسنات) is on the man's right, and the writer of evil deeds (كاتب السيئات) is on his left. The companion of the right (صاحب اليمين) is an authority (امير) over the companion of the left (صاحب الشمال). So, if he performs a good deed, the angel of the right (ملك اليمين) writes it as ten. And if he performs an evil deed, the companion of the right says to the companion of the left: "Leave him for seven hours, perhaps he will glorify [Allah] (يسبّح) or seek forgiveness (يستغفر)." In al-Kafi, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him), from him صلى الله عليه وآله: What is close to it and what is derived from it is that both of them are two writing angels (ملكان كاتبان). Perhaps the two writers are other than the commander (الآمر) and the restrainer (الزاجر).

(19) ** وَجاءَتْ سَكْرَةُ الْمَوْتِ بِالْحَقِ ** (And the agony of death comes with the truth) When He mentioned their deeming the Resurrection far-fetched and removed that doubt by confirming His power and His knowledge, He informed them that they will soon meet that (reality) at the time of death and the establishment of the Hour. And He drew attention to its proximity by expressing it using the past tense form. And سكرة الموت (the agony of death) is its severity which removes the intellect.

In al-Majma', regarding the variant readings (الشواذ): وجاءت سكرة الحقّ بالموت (And the agony of the truth came with death). He said: And our companions (أصحابنا) narrated it from the Imams of Guidance (أئمّة الهدى) (peace be upon them).

And al-Qummi said: It was revealed as وجاءت سكرة الحق بالموت (And the agony of the truth came with death). ذلِكَ ما كُنْتَ مِنْهُ تَحِيدُ ** (That is what you were shunning) You incline away and flee from it, and the address is to man. Al-Qummi said: It was revealed concerning the first one (الأوّل).

(٢٠) وَنُفِخَ فِي الصُّورِ ** (And the Trumpet will be blown) means the Resurrection (البعث). ذلِكَ يَوْمُ الْوَعِيدِ ** (That is the Day of the promise) [means] the day when the threat (الوعيد) is realized and fulfilled (وإنجازه).

(٢١) وَجاءَتْ كُلُّ نَفْسٍ مَعَها سائِقٌ وَشَهِيدٌ. (And every soul shall come, accompanied by a driver and a witness.) In Nahj al-Balaghah: ** سائِقٌ (a driver) drives it to its place of gathering (محشرها), and a witness (شاهد) testifies against it concerning its deeds (بعملها).

(٢٢) لَقَدْ كُنْتَ فِي غَفْلَةٍ مِنْ هذا ** (Certainly, you were oblivious of this) [is understood] upon the suppression (إضمار) of the saying [i.e., it is said to the soul]. فَكَشَفْنا عَنْكَ غِطاءَكَ ** (So We removed your covering from you) The covering (الغطاء) is that which obstructs the matters of the Hereafter (أمور المعاد), and it is the heedlessness (الغفلة), the immersion (الانهماك) in sensory perceptions (المحسوسات), the familiarity (الالف) with them, and the limitation (قصور) of one's view to them. فَبَصَرُكَ الْيَوْمَ حَدِيدٌ ** (and so your eyesight is sharp today.) [means] penetrating, due to the removal of the impediment (المانع) to sight (للابصار).

(٢٣) وَقالَ قَرِينُهُ ** (23) And his companion will say**

It was said: The angel appointed over him (الملك الموكّل عليه), or the devil who was assigned to him (الشيطان الذي قيّض له). And Al-Qummi (القمّيّ) (said) that his devil (شيطانه) is the second (interpretation).

And in al-Majma', narrated from both of them (peace be upon them): It means the witnessing angel (الملك الشهيد) against him.

** هذا ما لَدَيَّ عَتِيدٌ ** (This is what is ready with me)

This is what is written with me, present with me (حاضر لديّ), or this is what is with me and in my possession (ملكيتي), which I prepared for Hell through my temptation (إغوائي) and misguidance (إضلالي).

(٢٤) أَلْقِيا فِي جَهَنَّمَ كُلَّ كَفَّارٍ عَنِيدٍ ** (24) (Cast into hell every ungrateful, rebellious one) It was said that this is an address from Allah to the driver (السّائق) and the witness (الشهيد). And al-Qummi (said it is) an address to the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم and Ali عليه السلام. And that is the saying of al-Sadiq عليه السلام: "Ali is the divider (قسيم) of Paradise and the Fire." And narrated from al-Sajjad, from his father, from his grandfather, the Commander of the Faithful عليه السلام, who said: The Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم said: "Indeed, when Allah, the Blessed and Exalted, gathers the people on the Day of Resurrection upon a single plain, I and you will be that day on the right side of the Throne. Then Allah, the Blessed and Exalted, will say to me and to you: 'Arise, and cast into the Fire whoever hated you both and whoever denied you both.'" And in al-Majma' and al-Amali, through the path of the general populace (العامّة), there is a similar narration, with the addition: "And admit into Paradise whoever loved you both." And that is His saying, the Almighty: أَلْقِيا فِي جَهَنَّمَ كُلَّ كَفَّارٍ عَنِيدٍ. (Cast into hell every ungrateful, rebellious one.) And in another narration in al-Amali, he said: "It was revealed concerning me and concerning you, O son of Abu Talib" — the rest of the hadith.

(٢٥) مَنَّاعٍ لِلْخَيْرِ One who frequently withholds wealth from its obligatory rights مُعْتَدٍ transgressing مُرِيبٍ doubtful concerning Allah and concerning His religion. (٢٦) الَّذِي جَعَلَ مَعَ اللهِ إِلهاً آخَرَ فَأَلْقِياهُ فِي الْعَذابِ الشَّدِيدِ. (Who set up another god besides Allah. So cast him into the severe punishment.) (٢٧) قالَ قَرِينُهُ Meaning, the devil assigned to him رَبَّنا ما أَطْغَيْتُهُ As if the disbeliever said, "He made me rebellious," so his companion said, "I did not make him rebellious." وَلكِنْ كانَ فِي ضَلالٍ بَعِيدٍ So I assisted him in it, for the temptation (إغواء) of the devil only affects one who is unsound in judgment (مختلّ الرأي) and inclined towards wickedness (الفجور), as He said: وَما كانَ لِي عَلَيْكُمْ مِنْ سُلْطانٍ إِلَّا أَنْ دَعَوْتُكُمْ فَاسْتَجَبْتُمْ لِي ** (And I had no authority over you, except that I called you and you responded to me.) Al-Qummi said: The hinderer (المنّاع) is the second one, and لِلْخَيْرِ ** (of good) refers to the Wilayah (ولاية) of Ali (peace be upon him) and the rights of the Family of Muhammad (may the blessings of Allah be upon them). And when the first one wrote the document of Fadak to return it to Fatimah (peace be upon her), the second one prevented him, so he is مُعْتَدٍ مُرِيبٍ الَّذِي جَعَلَ مَعَ اللهِ إِلهاً آخَرَ He said: It is what he said: "We are disbelievers in the one who established for you the Imamate (الإمامة) and the Khums (الخمس)." As for His saying قالَ قَرِينُهُ ** (His companion will say), meaning his devil, and he is the second one, رَبَّنا ما أَطْغَيْتُهُ ** (Our Lord! I did not make him rebellious), meaning the first one.

(٢٨) قالَ لا تَخْتَصِمُوا لَدَيَ وَقَدْ قَدَّمْتُ إِلَيْكُمْ بِالْوَعِيدِ ** (28) He will say, 'Do not dispute before Me, while I had already warned you.' He said (i.e., Allah): Do not dispute before Me, meaning, in the station of accountability (موقف الحساب), for there is no benefit in it. while I had already warned you concerning transgression, in My Books and through the tongues of My Messengers, so no argument (حجّة) remains for you.

(٢٩) ما يُبَدَّلُ الْقَوْلُ لَدَيَ وَما أَنَا بِظَلَّامٍ لِلْعَبِيدِ ** (29) The word is not changed with Me, and I am not tyrannical to the servants.' The word is not changed with Me by the occurrence of contradiction (الخلف) in it. And the pardoning of some sinners for certain reasons is not part of the changing (التبديل), because it only occurs concerning one for Whom He decreed the pardon. Thus, that too is among what is not changed with Him. and I am not tyrannical to the servants,** so that I would punish one whom I have no right to punish.

(30) ** يَوْمَ نَقُولُ لِجَهَنَّمَ ** (The day We will say to Hell) And it was recited with the yāʾ (بالياء) [i.e., yaqūlu] ** هَلِ امْتَلَأْتِ وَتَقُولُ هَلْ مِنْ مَزِيدٍ ** (‘Are you full?’ And it will say, ‘Is there any more?’) It was said that the question and answer are brought forth for the sake of imagery (لِلتَّخْيِيل) and depiction (وَالتَّصْوِير). And the meaning is that despite its vastness, the Jinn (الجنّ) and mankind are cast into it group after group until it is full, due to His saying: لَأَمْلَأَنَّ جَهَنَّمَ (I will surely fill Hell). And that it is of such vastness (السعة) that whoever enters it, enters it, and yet there remains emptiness (فراغ) afterwards. And that due to the intensity of its roaring (زفيرها), its sharpness (حدّتها), and its clinging to the disobedient (العصاة), it is as if it is seeking more of them (كَالْمُسْتَكْثِر لَهُمْ) and demanding their increase (وَالطَّالِب لِزِيَادَتِهِم).

And al-Qummi said: It is an interrogative (استفهام) because Allah promised the Fire that He would fill it, so the Fire becomes full. Then He says to it: هَلِ امْتَلَأْتِ (Are you full?), and it says: هَلْ مِنْ مَزِيدٍ (Is there any more?), in the manner of an interrogation, meaning: there is no more (ليس في مزيد).

He said: Then Paradise (الجنّة) says: "O Lord, You promised the Fire that You would fill it, and You promised me that You would fill me, but You have not filled me, while You have filled the Fire."

He said: So on that Day, Allah creates a creation (خلقاً) and fills Paradise with them.

Then Abu Abd Allah (peace be upon him) said: "Blessedness (طُوبى) is for them; they did not see the sorrows (غموم) and worries (همومها) of the world."

(٣١) وَأُزْلِفَتِ الْجَنَّةُ لِلْمُتَّقِينَ ** (31) And Paradise is brought near for the Godwary, It is brought near for them غَيْرَ بَعِيدٍ ** a place that is not far. Al-Qummi said: أُزْلِفَتْ (is brought near) means it is adorned (زيّنت). غَيْرَ بَعِيدٍ (not far), he said, means quickly (بسرعة).

(٣٢) هذا ما تُوعَدُونَ ** (32) ‘This is what you were promised, [This is said] based on the omission of the verb of saying (إضمار القول). It was also recited with the yā’. لِكُلِّ أَوَّابٍ ** for every one who returns frequently (رجّاع) to Allah. This is a substitute for لِلْمُتَّقِينَ ** by repeating the preposition. حَفِيظٍ one who guards (حافظ) His limits (لحدوده).

(٣٣) مَنْ خَشِيَ الرَّحْمنَ بِالْغَيْبِ وَجاءَ بِقَلْبٍ مُنِيبٍ. (33) for everyone who is penitent and watchful, who fears the All-beneficent in secret and comes with a penitent heart.’

(٣٤) ادْخُلُوها ** (34) ‘Enter it in peace! It will be said to them: ادْخُلُوها بِسَلامٍ ** safe from the punishment and the cessation of blessings, or with peace (مسلّماً) upon you from Allah and His angels. ذلِكَ يَوْمُ الْخُلُودِ. That is the Day of Eternity.

(٣٥) لَهُمْ ما يَشاؤُنَ فِيها وَلَدَيْنا مَزِيدٌ ** (35) They will have whatever they wish therein, and with Us there is yet more. And that is what does not occur to their minds, of what no eye has seen, nor ear has heard, nor has crossed the heart of a human being (بشر). Al-Qummi said: [The increase (مَزِيدٌ)] is looking upon the Mercy of Allah. A narration concerning the meaning of this verse has already passed in Surat al-Sajdah and Luqman (peace be upon him).

(٣٦) ** وَكَمْ أَهْلَكْنا قَبْلَهُمْ ** (36) How many a generation We have destroyed before them before your people مِنْ قَرْنٍ هُمْ أَشَدُّ مِنْهُمْ بَطْشاً ** who were mightier than them in power! in strength, like 'Ād and Thamūd. فَنَقَّبُوا فِي الْبِلادِ They traveled through the lands. So they traversed the lands (فَخَرَقُوا الْبِلَادَ) and acted freely within them, or they roamed throughout the earth in every direction (كُلّ مَجَال). And the origin of al-tanqīb (التنقيب) is the searching (التنقير) for a thing and seeking it out (وَالْبَحْثُ عَنْهُ). هَلْ مِنْ مَحِيصٍ ** Was there any refuge? for them from Allah or from death.

(٣٧) إِنَّ فِي ذلِكَ لَذِكْرى لِمَنْ كانَ لَهُ قَلْبٌ ** (37) There is indeed a reminder in that for someone who has a heart, meaning a conscious heart that reflects upon the realities (الحقائق). In al-Kafi, narrated from al-Kazim (peace be upon him) in the tradition (حديث) of Hisham: It means intellect (عقل). ** أَوْ أَلْقَى السَّمْعَ ** or lends an ear, or listens attentively to hear it, وَهُوَ شَهِيدٌ ** while he is present. present with his mind so that he may understand its meanings. And in the indefiniteness (تنكير) and ambiguity (إبهامه) of the heart (القلب) there is magnification (تفخيم) and an indication that every heart which does not reflect and does not contemplate is as if it were no heart at all (كلا قلب). In al-Ma'ani, narrated from the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him): "I am the possessor of the heart (ذو القلب)," then He recited this verse in a tradition (حديث) of His.

(38) وَلَقَدْ خَلَقْنَا السَّماواتِ وَالْأَرْضَ وَما بَيْنَهُما فِي سِتَّةِ أَيَّامٍ ** (And We certainly created the heavens and the earth and what is between them in six days) Its exegesis has passed repeatedly. ** وَما مَسَّنا مِنْ لُغُوبٍ ** (and no fatigue touched Us) meaning, from weariness (تعب) and exhaustion (اعياء). This is a refutation of what the Jews claimed: that He, the Exalted, began the creation of the world on Sunday, finished it on Friday, rested on Saturday, and reclined upon the Throne (العرش).

In Rawdat al-Wa'izin (روضة الواعظين), it is narrated: The Jews came to the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله and asked him about the creation of the heavens and the earth. He said: "Allah created the earth on Sunday and Monday, and He created the mountains and what is within them on Tuesday, and on Wednesday He created the trees, the water, the cities (المدائن), the inhabited lands (العمران), and the ruins (الخراب). On Thursday He created the heaven (السماء), and on Friday He created the stars, the sun, the moon, and the angels." The Jews said: "Then what, O Muhammad?" He said: ثُمَّ اسْتَوى عَلَى الْعَرْشِ ** (Then He settled on the Throne) They said: "You have spoken correctly, if only you had completed it." They said: "Then He rested." The Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله became intensely angry, and so the verse وَلَقَدْ خَلَقْنَا ** was revealed.

(٣٩) فَاصْبِرْ عَلى ما يَقُولُونَ ** (39) So be patient with what they say What the polytheists say regarding describing the Truth (الحقّ) with that which is unfitting for His Majesty. وَسَبِّحْ بِحَمْدِ رَبِّكَ (And glorify your Lord with His praise). And declare Him far removed from the description that necessitates anthropomorphism (التشبيه), praising Him for what He has bestowed upon you, such as attaining the Truth (الحقّ) and other things. قَبْلَ طُلُوعِ الشَّمْسِ وَقَبْلَ الْغُرُوبِ (before sunrise and before sunset). Meaning al-Fajr and al-Asr. And the excellence (فضيلة) of these two times has already passed.

(٤٠) وَمِنَ اللَّيْلِ فَسَبِّحْهُ ** (40) And in the night, glorify Him, And glorify Him during a part of the night وَأَدْبارَ السُّجُودِ ** and after the prostrations. And after the prayers (أعقاب الصلاة). And it was read with a kasrah (i.e., وَإِدْبارَ) from (أدبرت الصلاة) when the prayer concluded.

In al-Majma', narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): He was asked about this Ayah, and He said: "You say, when you enter the morning and when you enter the evening, ten times: لا الهَ الّا اللهُ وَحْدَهُ لا شَرِيكَ لَهُ لَهُ الْمُلْكُ وَلَهُ الْحَمْدُ يُحْيِي وَيُمِيتُ وَهُوَ عَلى كُلِّ شَيْءٍ قَدِيرٌ (There is no god except Allah, alone, He has no partner; to Him belongs the dominion, and to Him belongs the praise; He gives life and causes death, and He has power over all things)."

And in al-Kafi, narrated from Imam al-Baqir (peace be upon him): He was asked about the Almighty's saying: وَأَدْبارَ السُّجُودِ , and He said: "Two rak'ahs after the Maghrib prayer."

And similar to this is narrated in al-Majma' from the Prophet and the Commander of the Faithful (may the blessings of Allah be upon him and his family) and al-Hasan al-Mujtaba (peace be upon him).

And al-Qummi narrated from Imam al-Rida (peace be upon him): He said: "Four rak'ahs after the Maghrib prayer."

And in al-Majma', narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): It is the Witr prayer at the end of the night.

(٤١) وَاسْتَمِعْ يَوْمَ يُنادِ الْمُنادِ ** (41) And listen on the day when the caller calls out It was said: [This refers] to the Resurrection (البعث) and the separation of judgment (فصل القضاء). And al-Qummi said: The caller (المنادي) will call out with the name of the Qā’im (القائم) and the name of his father (peace be upon them both). مِنْ مَكانٍ قَرِيبٍ ** (41) from a nearby place. such that His call reaches everyone equally. (٤٢) يَوْمَ يَسْمَعُونَ الصَّيْحَةَ بِالْحَقِ ** (42) The day they hear the Cry in truth: that is the day of emergence. Al-Qummi said: [This refers to] the cry (صيحة) of the Qā’im (القائم) from the heaven. ذلِكَ يَوْمُ الْخُرُوجِ. Al-Qummi narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him) that He said: It is the Return (الرجعة). (٤٣) إِنَّا نَحْنُ نُحْيِي وَنُمِيتُ ** (43) Indeed, it is We who give life and bring death, in the world (الدنيا). وَإِلَيْنَا الْمَصِيرُ ** (43) and toward Us is the destination. in the Hereafter (الآخرة). (٤٤) يَوْمَ تَشَقَّقُ ** (44) The day the earth splits open for them, (تتشقّق) (it splits open) and it was recited with the lightening (بالتخفيف) [of the tā’]. الْأَرْضُ عَنْهُمْ سِراعاً ** (44) [and they come out] swiftly. hastening. ذلِكَ حَشْرٌ ** (44) That is a gathering resurrection (بعث) and gathering (جمع). عَلَيْنا يَسِيرٌ ** (44) easy for Us. easy. Al-Qummi said: [This refers] to the Return (الرجعة). (٤٥) نَحْنُ أَعْلَمُ بِما يَقُولُونَ ** (45) We know best what they say, [This is] a consolation for the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم and a threat to them. وَما أَنْتَ عَلَيْهِمْ بِجَبَّارٍ (45) and you are not a tyrant over them. as one given authority (بمسلّط) who compels them to believe or does with them whatever you wish. Rather, you are only a caller (داع). **فَذَكِّرْ بِالْقُرْآنِ مَنْ يَخافُ وَعِيدِ ** (45) So admonish with the Qur’ān whosoever fears My threat. for none other than him benefits from it.

In Thawāb al-A‘māl (ثواب الأعمال) and al-Majma‘ (المجمع), narrated from Imam al-Baqir (peace be upon him): Whoever persists in reciting Sūrat Qāf (سورة ق) in his obligatory prayers (فرائضه) and supererogatory prayers (نوافله), Allah will expand his provision (رزقه), give him his book in his right hand, and reckon him with an easy reckoning (حساباً يسيراً).
